title: Peppery American Cocoa Beverage
description: A rich and energizing hot drink, perfect for a cold winter morning. This recipe is a twist on traditional hot chocolate, with a spicy kick from cayenne pepper.
instructions: Combine the cocoa powder, water, and milk in a ttukbaegi earthenware pot.
Whisk the mixture until the cocoa powder is dissolved.
Add the honey, cayenne pepper, salt, black pepper, vanilla extract, and nutmeg to the pot.
Place the pot over medium heat and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook for 5 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together.
Remove the pot from the heat and add the butter.
Whisk until the butter is melted.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Pour the hot cocoa into mugs and serve immediately.
Garnish with a sprinkle of nutmeg, if desired.
Serve immediately, while the drink is still hot.
Store any leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
Reheat the leftovers by placing the pot over low heat and whisking until hot and steaming.
Serve the peppery American cocoa beverage in a large mug.
Garnish with a sprinkle of cocoa powder or a cinnamon stick, if desired.
Serve as a pick-me-up after a long day.
Enjoy the warm and comforting flavors of this peppery drink.
